title: aristolochia oblongata subsp. oblongata
intro: Aristolochia oblongata subsp. oblongata, also known as the Himalayan snake root, is a sub-species of flowering plant in the Aristolochia genus, characterized by its heart-shaped leaves and yellowish-green flowers. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of aristolochic acid, which has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and snake bites. The medicinal properties of Aristolochia oblongata subsp. oblongata have been reported to have anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral effects, contributing to its potential health benefits, such as reducing pain and swelling, and supporting immune function. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, or used in the form of dried roots or powdered extracts, which are then used to treat a range of health conditions.
